comments: Time at start of experiment is in the Neuralynx events file. Recorded with 40 kHz sample rate and no high-pass filter. Not sure whether headstage was bad or not yet.

Iso for tail vein cannulation was stopped at 1:22 pm, >=1 hr before the start of the baseline recording. No clave; used luer-lock cap. Recorded with 40 kHz sample rate and no high-pass filter. Not sure whether headstage was bad or not yet.

Iso for tail vein cannulation was stopped at 1:08 pm, >=1 hr before the start of the baseline recording. No clave; used luer-lock cap. Ketamine dilution was made by putting 0.6 mL of 100 mg/mL ketamine in 9.4 mL saline. Computer suddenly shut off when I tried to remote desktop in (I was having bad allergies). This was just after the infusion finished. As a result, the .ncs files won’t open with Python Neo. Recorded with 40 kHz sample rate and no high-pass filter. Not sure whether headstage was bad or not yet.

Iso for tail vein cannulation was stopped at 1:17 pm, >=1 hr before the start of the baseline recording. No clave; used luer-lock cap. Dexmedetomidine dilution was made by putting 0.2 mL of 0.1 mg/mL dexmedetomidine in 9.8 mL saline. Recorded with 40 kHz sample rate. Used bad headstage.

Iso for tail vein cannulation was stopped at 1:16 pm, >=1 hr before the start of the baseline recording. No clave; used luer-lock cap. Ketamine dilution was made by putting 0.6 mL of 100 mg/mL ketamine in 9.4 mL saline. Recorded with 40 kHz sample rate. Used bad headstage.

Iso for tail vein cannulation was stopped at 1:42 pm, >=1 hr before the start of the baseline recording. No clave; used luer-lock cap. Computer suddenly shut off at the end of the experiment. I was not doing anything to cause it. As a result, the .ncs files won’t open with Python Neo. Recorded with 40 kHz sample rate. Used bad headstage.

Iso for tail vein cannulation was stopped at 1:39 pm, >=1 hr before the start of the baseline recording. No clave; used luer-lock cap. Ketamine dilution was made by putting 0.93 mL of 100 mg/mL ketamine in 14.57 mL saline. Used bad headstage. I think this was the first experiment during which I used SAI infusion tubing EXT-60, with 1.4 mL of dead space.

Iso for tail vein cannulation was stopped at 1:32 pm, >=1 hr before the start of the baseline recording. No clave; used luer-lock cap. Dexmedetomidine dilution was made by putting 1 mL of 0.1 mg/mL dexmedetomidine in 19 mL saline. Forgot to remove IV tubing until a while after the infusion finished (but before the rat righted herself). Used bad headstage. Used SAI infusion tubing EXT-60, with 1.4 mL of dead space.

Iso for tail vein cannulation was stopped at 1:56 pm, >=1 hr before the start of the baseline recording. No clave; used luer-lock cap. Dexmedetomidine dilution was made by putting 0.3 mL of 0.1 mg/mL dexmedetomidine in 14.7 mL saline. Used SAI infusion tubing EXT-60, with 1.4 mL of dead space. Iso for tail vein cannulation was stopped at 2:01 pm, >=1 hr before the start of the baseline recording. No clave; used luer-lock cap. Dexmedetomidine dilution was made by putting 0.75 mL of 0.1 mg/mL dexmedetomidine in 14.25 mL saline. There was a puddle under the rat by the time the infusion stopped. The rat was quite unconscious, so I assume it was urine, and not dexmedetomidine.

CNO was given at 1:41 pm, ~1 hr before the start of the ketamine infusion. No clave; used luer-lock cap. Ketamine dilution was made by putting 2.4 mL of 100 mg/mL ketamine in 37.6 mL saline. Heating pad was already started before recording.
